items.reason: The routing index now points logging sampling questions to the new log-sampler design.
The logging module overview now includes unified random log sampling, LogSampler sources, and the access recorder facade guidance.
The new design doc covers request/access/diagnostic sample rates, derivation, propagation, hot-path constraints, APIs, and tests.
The obsolete standalone-client DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT environment variable entry is removed because sampling is worker-authoritative.
The log guide replaces log_rate_limit with request/access/diagnostic sampling behavior, derivation rules, and configuration examples.
The dscli configuration reference replaces log_rate_limit with the three new sampling rates and explains derivation semantics.
This canonical logging design still documents log_rate_limit, DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT, LogRateLimiter, and old register propagation behavior.
The trace context doc still says SetRequestTraceUUID and SetTraceContext create decisions when local log_rate_limit is enabled and names LogRateLimiter.
The access recorder doc was not updated for the new Object/Stream/RequestOut facades, lazy sampled-out skipping, or access_sample_rate semantics.
This PLOG design still frames slow diagnostics around log_rate_limit and skipping LogRateLimiter rather than the new diagnostic sampling model.
This implementation summary still says PLOG bypasses log_rate_limit request sampling and was not reconciled with LogSampler diagnostics.
